questions.subject: Steel Structures
questions.stem: A tension member of gross area $1500\,\text{mm}^2$ (Fe$250$, $\gamma_{m0}=1.1$) has design strength governed by gross yielding $=\dfrac{A_g f_y}{\gamma_{m0}}$ = _____ kN.

questions.subject: Steel Structures
questions.stem: A tension member of gross area $2000\,\text{mm}^2$ (Fe$250$, $\gamma_{m0}=1.1$) has design strength governed by gross yielding $=\dfrac{A_g f_y}{\gamma_{m0}}$ = _____ kN.

questions.subject: Steel Structures
questions.stem: The design shear strength of a bearing-type bolt (single shear) is given (LSM) by $\dfrac{f_{ub} A_{nb}}{\sqrt 3\,\gamma_{mb}}$. The factor $\sqrt 3$ arises from
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shear yield = $f_y/\sqrt3$ (von Mises).

**Working.**
$\sqrt3$ from von Mises.

**Answer.** von Mises relation

questions.subject: Steel Structures
questions.stem: Which limit states govern the design tensile strength of a steel plate with bolt holes?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tension members: yielding, rupture and block shear. LTB is a flexural limit state.

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 3.

**Answer.** Yielding, rupture, block shear
